title: Strengths to preserve

items.title: Non-claims as a first-class field
items.summary: Every item record carries explicit non_claims (64 entries across 23 nodes). This guards downstream prose and LLM composition against silent overclaim drift.
items.preserve_because: Removes a major risk class (positive overclaim by omission) at schema level, not at policy level.
items.regression_signal_if_lost: non_claims becoming optional, deduplicated only globally, or stripped from derived views.

items.title: Eight-level proof_level ladder
items.summary: proof_level enum (none → source-anchor → static-check → unit-test → integration-test → runtime-smoke → real-document-proof → production-observation) forces claims to declare the highest currently earned evidence, not aspirational level.
items.preserve_because: Makes proof granularity machine-readable and forces honest downgrades when evidence is bounded.
items.regression_signal_if_lost: Records marked validated without matching runtime/real-document proof, or collapsed ladder.

items.title: ADR-style decision metadata enforced by schema
items.summary: Schema requires deciders, decision_drivers, and considered_options for any item.type=decision via allOf/if-then. DEC-D031 and DEC-D032 fully populate pros/cons, consequences, assumptions, constraints, implications.
items.preserve_because: Prevents context-free decisions and supports later review/supersession traceability.
items.regression_signal_if_lost: Decision records without consequences or considered_options passing the schema.

items.title: Fail-closed verifier policy with deterministic diagnostics
items.summary: scripts/verify-architecture-graph.py composes S02 --check and S03 --check, then applies schema, anchor freshness, missing-endpoint, orphan, gate-metadata, decision-fitness, contradiction, and overclaim-scan rules. Failures are fail-closed and report rule + record ID + field.
items.preserve_because: Deterministic, diagnostic-friendly default for an artifact class that must not silently drift.
items.regression_signal_if_lost: Verifier downgraded to warnings, custom-path runs treated as evidence, or overclaim scan removed.

items.title: Schema-level safeguards against unsafe content
items.summary: path_string forbids absolute paths via not.pattern=^/; identifier enforces uppercase-prefixed pattern; quote_hash uses hex regex; superseded items must point at superseded_by; minItems=1 for source_anchors when generated_draft is false.
items.preserve_because: Catches whole classes of authoring errors (absolute paths, missing anchors, dangling supersession) before any graph rule fires.
items.regression_signal_if_lost: Loosened required arrays, removed regex constraints, or weakened allOf branches.

items.title: Source-of-truth boundary repeated in every derived view
items.summary: README, architecture_report.md, architecture_health.md, product_readiness_blockers.md, and claims_ledger.md each explicitly state that they are derived, non-authoritative projections and that PRD/GSD/ADR/source/runtime evidence remains authoritative.
items.preserve_because: Boundary discipline is repeated at every consumption point, reducing the chance that a downstream agent treats a derived view as authority.
items.regression_signal_if_lost: Derived view introducing positive product claims, or removing the non-authoritative disclaimer.

items.title: Separation of pipeline stages S02 / S03 / S04 / views
items.summary: Extractor, graph builder, verifier, and views generator are independent scripts with explicit --check freshness modes. Each stage owns specific output paths and contracts.
items.preserve_because: Allows independent evolution and isolates the failure cause when a stage drifts.
items.regression_signal_if_lost: Stages merging into a single script, or views generated without freshness gate.

non_claims: Strengths describe registry mechanics, not product architecture quality.
Preserving these strengths does not validate runtime behavior.
